metadata.title: Measure the Context Cost of Your MCP Server Stack with Claude

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Hola, Liam — in for Bear. Agentic CLI — build it, measure it, take it apart? Can you explain it, Bear?

props.command: Agentic CLI — build it, measure it, take it apart?
props.segment: Measure the Context Cost of Your

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Agentic CLI — build it, measure it, take it apart.

Every MCP server you leave connected adds a schema cost before your first message — and you're probably paying it on servers you haven't used in weeks. Eight servers connected: GitHub, Slack, Drive, database, web search, calendar, Jira, email. Your starting context is already loaded with tool descriptions you didn't ask for.

shot.notes: title card: MCP server icons in a row, each with a small token cost badge; running total at top reads 'schema tax: X tokens before message 1'

beats.narration_text: Ask for the cost meter. In the terminal: claude — read these MCP server tool descriptions, estimate the schema token cost per server, classify each as always-needed, project-needed, or one-off, and flag servers safe to disable.

props.command:
claude "Read these MCP server tool descriptions and
  estimate the schema token cost for each.
  Classify: always-needed / project-needed / one-off.
  Flag servers safe to disable." < mcp_config.json

beats.narration_text: Read the script before trusting it. It reads the MCP configuration JSON, extracts tool descriptions, uses a word-count proxy for token cost, sorts servers by schema weight, and classifies each by the pattern of tools — database connectors are project-needed, email is almost always one-off.

props.code:
# mcp_cost_meter.py — Claude Code output
import json, sys

def word_tokens(text):
    return int(len(text.split()) * 1.33)

config = json.load(open('mcp_config.json'))
servers = config.get('mcpServers', {})
ledger = []
for name, cfg in servers.items():
    desc = cfg.get('description', '') + ' '.join(
        str(t) for t in cfg.get('tools', []))
    cost = word_tokens(desc)
    ledger.append({'name': name, 'tokens': cost})
ledger.sort(key=lambda x: -x['tokens'])
for row in ledger:
    print(f"{row['name']:20s}  {row['tokens']:5d} tokens")

beats.narration_text: The ledger appears: Jira tops the list at twelve hundred tokens — it ships with forty-two tool descriptions. Email and calendar follow. GitHub, the tool you actually use every day, is mid-table. Web search: eighty tokens. Always-needed. Jira: flagged as one-off.

shot.notes: ranked cost ledger: server names left column, token cost bar right column, classification badge (green=always-needed, yellow=project-needed, red=one-off); running total token counter at top

beats.narration_text: Now remove the one-off servers and re-run. In the terminal: claude — update mcp_cost_meter.py, filter out one-off servers from the config, and show me the new starting token total.

props.command:
claude "update mcp_cost_meter.py:
  -> remove all one-off servers from config
  -> show new starting token total
  -> compare before vs after"

beats.narration_text: The total drops from four thousand two hundred tokens to one thousand eight hundred. Sixty percent of your starting context weight, gone. The remaining servers are the minimum viable surface — the tools that are on the field for this project type.

shot.notes: before/after comparison: two stacked bars — 4200 tokens (red, 8 servers) above, 1800 tokens (green, 3 servers) below; 'starting context weight -60%' annotation

beats.narration_text: Schema cost is a tax you pay before the work begins. Disabling a server you haven't used this month costs nothing and immediately reduces the reasoning overhead on every prompt you send. The schema is not free because the server is idle — it is there in the context window whether you call it or not.

shot.notes: teardown card: 'schema cost = tax before work begins'; bullet: 'idle server still loads its schema'; bullet: 'disabling costs zero, saves on every prompt'

beats.narration_text: Your move: export your actual MCP config and run this meter. Sort by cost and review anything classified as one-off. Next reel: we build the folder structure auditor — because what the agent reads before it starts is the other half of the problem.

shot.notes: next steps card: '1) export your mcp_config.json  2) run cost meter  3) disable one-off servers'; teaser: 'next: folder structure auditor'

beats.narration_text: Here's what the body just demonstrated. Schema cost is a tax you pay before the work begins. Disabling a server you haven't used this month costs nothing and immediately reduces the reasoning overhead on every prompt you send. The schema is not free because the server is idle — it is there in the context window whether you call it or not. Your move: export your actual MCP config and run this meter. beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Measure the Context Cost of Your MCP Server Stack with Claude] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
